channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump says ISIS chief killed in Iraq
item.description: President Trump on Friday announced that ISIS’s second in command was killed in Iraq this week, touting it as an example of his administration’s “peace through strength” military philosophy.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Large crowds converge in Belgrade for protest rally against Serbia's president and government
item.description: Crowds of protesters are gathering in Belgrade for a massive rally in the Serbian capital seen as a culmination of months-long protests against populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: Jovana Gec And Dusan Stojanovic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Trump is singling out South Africa and accusing it of being anti-white and anti-American
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to expel the South African ambassador is its latest move against a country it has singled out for sanctions and accused of being anti-white and anti-American.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuba suffers another massive power outage leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Cuba has suffered a massive power outage affecting Havana and the country’s provinces that has left millions in the dark.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. military intervention versus cartels "impossible," says Mexico's president
item.description: "He said everything’s on the table, well no, not everything is on the table, nor on the chair, nor on the floor, nor anywhere, not that.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, WGN 9

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Thai officials after Uyghur men are deported to China
item.description: The U.S. State Department has announced sanctions on an unknown number of current and former Thai officials for their role in deporting at least 40 Uyghur men to China despite worries that they could be jailed or even face death there.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte appears by video link at International Criminal Court hearing
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Jailed former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte appeared Friday by videoconference before judges at the International Criminal Court, days after his arrest in Manila on murder charges linked to the deadly “ war on drugs ” that he oversaw while in office.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Millions of people celebrate Holi, the Hindu festival of colors
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Millions of people in South Asia celebrated Holi, the Hindu festival of colors, on Friday by smearing each other with brightly colored powder, dancing to festive music and feasting on traditional sweets prepared for the occasion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP promotes use of 'Taiwan' over 'Chinese Taipei' in slight to Beijing
item.description: A group of House Republicans is introducing legislation to assert Taiwan as the official name of the independently-governed island in U.S. communication, pushing back against claims to the territory by the Chinese government in Beijing. Rep. Byron Donalds (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Waltz: Russia, Ukraine peace deal likely to include Donbas region cession
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz said Ukraine likely would have to cede some of its Donbas region to Russia if it wants a long-term peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Whipsawed by Trump's tariffs, the US public is getting a lot more nervous about the economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's volatile tariff threats are unleashing historic jumps in public anxiety, with the potential to undermine his pledges to strengthen a U.S. economy that is increasingly weakened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister
item.description: Carney, a former Goldman Sachs executive with no experience in politics, becomes Canada's 24th prime minister.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN report warns Iran is stepping up electronic surveillance of women to enforce headscarf laws
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Iran is increasingly relying on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country's mandatory headscarf in public, as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN World Food Program to cut food aid for over 1 million people in Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The United Nations food agency said on Friday that more than 1 million people in the war-torn nation of Myanmar will be cut off from food assistance due to critical funding shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas agrees to release Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage
item.description: Hamas agreed Friday to release Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage in captivity in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Naples struck by 4.4-magnitude quake causing minor damage and light injuries
item.description: ROME (AP) — The southern Italian city of Naples was struck by a 4.4-magnitude quake early Thursday that caused only minor damage and sent 11 people to the hospital, the most serious suffering contusions after part of a ceiling collapsed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guatemala steps up patrols along border as US extends border security goals south
item.description: OCOS, Guatemala (AP) — As the United States government works to effectively extend its border security objectives south into Central America, countries like Guatemala have come under renewed scrutiny and pressure to step up their own border enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ontario Premier Ford hails 'positive' meeting with Lutnick
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lauded his Thursday meeting with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick as “positive” and “productive” after their public rift over tariffs on imported goods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Putin agrees in principle with proposal for Ukraine ceasefire and says more discussions are needed
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that he agrees in principle with a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine, but he emphasized that the terms are yet to be worked out and added that any truce should pave the way to lasting peace.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans think Trump is too close to Russia
item.description: A majority of Americans think President Trump is too closely aligned with Russia, including more than a quarter of Republicans, a poll released Thursday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas sent a toddler to a military outpost
item.description: Israeli soldiers spotted a 4-year-old child approaching their military outpost, who was apparently sent by Hamas. Israel says the boy was returned to Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is stable and showing improvements but will remain hospitalized
item.description: Pope Francis' health has taken a turn for the better this week as he continues to show improvement following a Monday update that listed him as “out of danger from death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Pressed to accept a ceasefire in Ukraine, Putin seems likely to seek his own conditions
item.description: By signaling its openness to a ceasefire, Ukraine has handed the Kremlin a difficult challenge at a time when the Russian military has the upper hand in the war: Should Moscow accept a truce and abandon hopes of making new gains, or should it reject the offer and risk derailing a cautious rapprochem
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Detained captain of cargo ship that collided with US oil tanker is Russian national, ship owner says
item.description: A Russian national was revealed to be the captain of the cargo ship that collided with an American tanker off the coast of England, spilling U.S. military jet fuel into the sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Pope follows Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as recovery continues
item.description: Pasolini delivered a meditation on “The hope of eternal life,” a theme that was chosen well before Francis was admitted to Rome's Gemelli hospital on Feb. 14 with a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation
